samples.xml_description:
<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data.</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="cnt">Counter to keep track of the number of serial input data bits.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <if>
                <condition>
                    <equals>
                        <attribute name="cnt"/>
                        <constant value="3'd7"/>
                    </equals>
                </condition>
                <then>
                    <assign>
                        <attribute name="dout_parallel"/>
                        <attribute name="din_serial"/>
                    </assign>
                    <assign>
                        <attribute name="dout_valid"/>
                        <constant value="1'b1"/>
                    </assign>
                </then>
                <else>
                    <assign>
                        <attribute name="dout_valid"/>
                        <constant value="1'b0"/>
                    </assign>
                </else>
            </if>
        </sequential_logic>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Converts 8-bit data input to 16-bit data output. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Input signal indicating the validity of the input data. 8-bit input data. Output signal indicating the validity of the output data. 16-bit output data. Flag signal for the width conversion process. Register to store the first valid input data. Flag signal to indicate when the second valid data is ready for concatenation.

Reset the data_out register to 0 when the reset signal is active (negedge rst_n).

Concatenate the data_lock and data_in registers to the data_out register when the input data is valid (valid_in) and the flag signal is active.

Set the flag signal to 1 when the input data is valid (valid_in) and the flag signal is not yet active.

Store the input data in the data_lock register when the input data is valid (valid_in).

samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal circumstances, the traffic light should cycle through green for 60 clock cycles, yellow for 5 clock cycles, and red for 10 clock cycles. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks, it should be shortened to 10 clocks. If the remaining green time is less than 10 clocks, it should remain unchanged.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button input.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum id="s1_red">State 1 - Red.</enum>
        <enum id="s2_yellow">State 2 - Yellow.</enum>
        <enum id="s3_green">State 3 - Green.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="state">
            <description>State transition logic.</description>
            <case>
                <item value="idle">Transition to s1_red.</item>
                <item value="s1_red">Transition to s3_green after a count of 60.</item>
                <item value="s2_yellow">Transition to s1_red after a count of 5.</item>
                <item value="s3_green">Transition to s2_yellow after a count of 10.</item>
            </case>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="cnt">
            <description>Counter logic.</description>
            <if>
                <true>Reset counter to 10.</true>
                <false>
                    <if>
                        <true>Increment counter if pass_request is active and green is on.</true>
                        <false>
                            <if>
                                <true>Set counter to 60 if green is off and p_green is on.</true>
                                <false>
                                    <if>
                                        <true>Set counter to 5 if yellow is off and p_yellow is on.</true>
                                        <false>
                                            <if>
                                                <true>Set counter to 10 if red is off and p_red is on.</true>
                                                <false>Decrement counter by 1.</false>
                                            </if>
                                        </false>
                                    </if>
                                </false>
                            </false>
                        </false>
                    </if>
                </false>
            </if>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal circumstances, the traffic light should cycle through green for 60 clock cycles, yellow for 5 clock cycles, and red for 10 clock cycles. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks, it should be shortened to 10 clocks. If the remaining green time is less than 10 clocks, it should remain unchanged.</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. Set to 8 to represent an 8-bit multiplier. Set to 7 to represent the number of bits minus one for an 8-bit multiplier. Register to store the value of mul_en_in for use in the next clock cycle. mul_en_in mul_en_out_reg assign Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. mul_en_out_reg[-1] mul_en_out assign Register to store the value of mul_a for use in the next clock cycle. mul_a mul_a_reg assign Register to store the value of mul_b for use in the next clock cycle. mul_b mul_b_reg assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[0]}} temp[0] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[1]}} temp[1] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[2]}} temp[2] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[3]}} temp[3] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[4]}} temp[4] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[5]}} temp[5] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[6]}} temp[6] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[7]}} temp[7] assign Register to store the value of the first sum for use in the next clock cycle. temp[0] sum[0] assign Register to store the value of the second sum for use in the next clock cycle. temp[1] + temp[2] sum[1] assign Register to store the value of the third sum for use in the next clock cycle. temp[3] + temp[4] sum[2] assign Register to store the value of the fourth sum for use in the next clock cycle. temp[5] + temp[6] sum[3] assign Register to store the value of the fifth sum for use in the next clock cycle. temp[7] sum[4] assign Register to store the value of the product for use in the next clock cycle. sum[0] + sum[1] + sum[2] + sum[3] + sum[4] mul_out_reg assign Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. mul_en_out ? mul_out_reg : 0 mul_out assign

Register the input enable signal mul_en_in in mul_en_out_reg on the positive edge of the clock signal. The output enable signal mul_en_out is derived from the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg, indicating whether the multiplication operation is enabled.

Register the input multiplicand and multiplier values in mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg, respectively, on the positive edge of the clock signal. These registers are only updated when the input enable signal is active.

Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. Store the partial products in a set of wires, temp, with each wire representing a different bit position.

Calculate partial sums by performing addition operations on the partial products. Store the intermediate sum values in a set of registers, sum, with each register corresponding to a group of partial products. Update the registers on the positive edge of the clock signal.

Calculate the final product by summing up all the partial sums. Store the accumulated sum in a register, mul_out_reg, on the positive edge of the clock signal.

Assign the output product value, mul_out, based on the output enable signal and the value in mul_out_reg. If the output enable signal is active, indicating a valid product, the value in mul_out_reg is assigned to mul_out. Otherwise, mul_out is set to 0.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a module for parallel-to-serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB). The output signal valid_out is set to 1 to indicate the availability of valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output sequentially in the following 3 cycles. Clock signal used for synchronous operations. Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. 4-bit parallel data input. Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output. Serial output representing the converted data. Counter register for tracking the serial output position. Valid signal for indicating the availability of serial output. Register to store the shifted data for serial output.

Parallel-to-serial conversion is performed by shifting the data and outputting the most significant bit (MSB) first.

When the counter (cnt) reaches 3 (indicating the last bit of the parallel input), the data register (data) is updated with the parallel input (d), and the valid signal (valid) is set to 1.

Otherwise, the counter (cnt) is incremented by 1, and the valid signal (valid) is set to 0.

The data register (data) is shifted one bit to the left, with the most significant bit (MSB) shifted to the least significant bit (LSB).

If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the counter (cnt) is reset to 0.

samples.xml_description:
Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding. 8-bit input operand A (multiplicand). 8-bit input operand B (multiplier). 16-bit output product. 8 3

Algorithm:

  1. Initialize
  2. Iterate i from 0 to b - 1.
  3. If the ith bit of B is set (if (B[i])), add A to the product at the position i (i.e., product[i] += A).

    Note:

    • The addition is meant to be carried out using a simple assignment for the sake of clarity, not bitwise addition with a carry-out.
    • The addition is carried out at the ith bit of the multiplier, not at the ith stage.
  4. Shift A left by one position (logical shift).

Example:

Example inputs: A = 0000 0010 (2) B = 0000 0011 (3) Initialization: product = 0000 0000 0000 0000 (0) Iteration 1 (i = 0): B[0] = 1, so add A to the product at position 0:
product = 0000 0000 0000 0010 (2) Iteration 2 (i = 1): B[1] = 1, so add A to the product at position 1:
product = 0000 0000 0000 0000 + 0000 0000 0000 0100 (4) = 0000 0000 0000 0100 (4) Iteration 3 (i = 2): B[2] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 2:
product = 0000 0000 0000 0100 (4) Iteration 4 (i = 3): B[3] = 1, so add A to the product at position 3:
product = 0000 0000 0000 0100 (4) + 0000 0000 0010 0000 (64) = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68) Shift A left by one position: A = 0000 0100 (4) << 1 = 0000 1000 (8) Iteration 5 (i = 4): B[4] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 4:
product = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68) Iteration 6 (i = 5): B[5] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 5:
product = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68) Iteration 7 (i = 6): B[6] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 6:
product = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68) Iteration 8 (i = 7): B[7] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 7:
product = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68)

Note:

  • The addition is carried out at the ith bit of the multiplier, not at the ith stage.
  • The multiplication algorithm is designed to prevent the addition from being carried out at the ith stage.

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Detect a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">Initial state of the FSM.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State of the FSM when the input is 10.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State of the FSM when the input is 100.</enum>
        <enum id="S4">State of the FSM when the input is 1001.</enum>
        <enum id="S5">State of the FSM when the input is 10011.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="MATCH" type="combinational_logic">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</reg>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic">Current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="combinational_logic">Next state of the FSM.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The module implements an FSM detection. On every change in the input signal (IN) or positive edge of CLK or RST, if RST is active, the output signal MATCH is set to 0. If the sequence of inputs IN is 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, the MATCH signal is 1 at the same time as the last occurrence of IN=1. If the sequence of inputs IN is 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, the MATCH signal becomes 1 at the fifth and ninth of IN; otherwise, it is set to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Design a frequency divider that divides the input clock frequency of 100MHz into 50MHz, 10MHz, and 1MHz. Input clock signal. Reset signal. Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2. Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10. Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100. Input clock signal. Reset signal. Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2. Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10. Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100. Counter for CLK_50. Counter for CLK_10. Counter for CLK_1. The module uses three counters to divide the input clock frequency.
  • CLK_50 generation: On every positive edge of CLK_in or RST, if RST is active, CLK_50 is set to 0. Otherwise, CLK_50 is toggled by inverting its current value.
  • CLK_10 generation: On every positive edge of CLK_in or RST, if RST is active, CLK_10 is set to 0, and the counter cnt_10 is reset to 0. If the counter cnt_10 reaches a value of 4, CLK_10 is toggled by inverting its current value, and the counter cnt_10 is reset to 0. Otherwise, the counter cnt_10 is incremented by 1.
  • CLK_1 generation: On every positive edge of CLK_in or RST, if RST is active, CLK_1 is set to 0, and the counter cnt_100 is reset to 0. If the counter cnt_100 reaches a value of 49, CLK_1 is toggled by inverting its current value, and the counter cnt_100 is reset to 0. Otherwise, the counter cnt_100 is incremented by 1.

<module id="alu">
    <description>Implement an ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">ALU control signal.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit output representing the result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">A 1-bit output indicating whether a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">A 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">A 1-bit output representing a general flag.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="ADD" value="6'b100000">ADD</enum>
        <enum id="ADDU" value="6'b100001">ADDU</enum>
        <enum id="SUB" value="6'b100010">SUB</enum>
        <enum id="SUBU" value="6'b100011">SUBU</enum>
        <enum id="AND" value="6'b100100">AND</enum>
        <enum id="OR" value="6'b100101">OR</enum>
        <enum id="XOR" value="6'b100110">XOR</enum>
        <enum id="NOR" value="6'b100111">NOR</enum>
        <enum id="SLT" value="6'b101010">SLT</enum>
        <enum id="SLTU" value="6'b101011">SLTU</enum>
        <enum id="SLL" value="6'b000000">SLL</enum>
        <enum id="SRL" value="6'b000010">SRL</enum>
        <enum id="SRA" value="6'b000011">SRA</enum>
        <enum id="SLLV" value="6'b000100">SLLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRLV" value="6'b000110">SRLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRAV" value="6'b000111">SRAV</enum>
        <enum id="LUI" value="6'b001111">LUI</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. 
The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result (r) to the lower 32 bits of the register (res[31:0]). The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.
Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal (aluc). The result is assigned to the register (res) accordingly. For shift operations (SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV), the shift amount is determined by the value of 'a' or 'a[4:0]'. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result.
If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).</implementation>
</module>

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="ZERO" description="State ZERO">0</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="ONE" description="State ONE">1</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">
            <name>i_state</name>
            <enum-value id="ZERO"/>
        </reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">
            <name>i_next_state</name>
            <enum-value id="ZERO"/>
        </reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <parablock>
            <statement>
                <text>Always block for updating state on positive edge of clk or negative edge of rst_n</text>
                <condition>
                    <or>
                        <and>
                            <input-ref id="clk"/>
                            <op>==</op>
                            <const>1'b1</const>
                        </and>
                        <and>
                            <input-ref id="rst_n"/>
                            <op>==</op>
                            <const>1'b0</const>
                        </and>
                    </or>
                </condition>
                <statement>
                    <if>
                        <condition>
                            <input-ref id="rst_n"/>
                            <op>==</op>
                            <const>1'b0</const>
                        </condition>
                        <statement>
                            <text>Reset condition: Set state to ZERO</text>
                            <assign>
                                <lhs>
                                    <reg-ref id="i_state"/>
                                </lhs>
                                <rhs>
                                    <enum-ref id="ZERO"/>
                                </rhs>
                            </assign>
                        </statement>
                        <else>
                            <statement>
                                <text>Update state based on next state</text>
                                <assign>
                                    <lhs>
                                        <reg-ref id="i_state"/>
                                    </lhs>
                                    <rhs>
                                        <reg-ref id="i_next_state"/>
                                    </rhs>
                                </assign>
                            </statement>
                        </else>
                    </if>
                </statement>
            </statement>
            <statement>
                <text>Always block for combinational logic to determine next state</text>
                <condition>
                    <input-ref id="clk"/>
                    <op>==</op>
                    <const>1'b0</const>
                </condition>
                <statement>
                    <text>Initialize next state to current state</text>
                    <assign>
                        <lhs>
                            <reg-ref id="i_next_state"/>
                        </lhs>
                        <rhs>
                            <reg-ref id="i_state"/>
                        </rhs>
                    </assign>
                </statement>
                <statement>
                    <text>Case statement for determining next state based on current state and input a</text>
                    <case>
                        <switch>
                            <reg-ref id="i_state"/>
                            <enum-value id="ZERO"/>
                        </switch>
                        <case-item>
                            <condition>
                                <and>
                                    <input-ref id="a"/>
                                    <op>==</op>
                                    <const>1'b1</const>
                                </and>
                            </condition>
                            <statement>
                                <text>Transition to state ONE</text>
                                <assign>
                                    <lhs>
                                        <reg-ref id="i_next_state"/>
                                    </lhs>
                                    <rhs>
                                        <enum-ref id="ONE"/>
                                    </rhs>
                                </assign>
                            </statement>
                        </case-item>
                        <case-item>
                            <condition>
                                <and>
                                    <input-ref id="a"/>
                                    <op>==</op>
                                    <const>1'b0</const>
                                </and>
                            </condition>
                            <statement>
                                <text>Transition to state ZERO</text>
                                <assign>
                                    <lhs>
                                        <reg-ref id="i_next_state"/>
                                    </lhs>
                                    <rhs>
                                        <enum-ref id="ZERO"/>
                                    </rhs>
                                </assign>
                            </statement>
                        </case-item>
                    </case>
                </statement>
            </statement>
            <statement>
                <text>Always block for generating output signals based on state and input a</text>
                <condition>
                    <input-ref id="clk"/>
                    <op>==</op>
                    <const>1'b1</const>
                </condition>
                <statement>
                    <text>Initialize rise and down output signals to 0</text>
                    <assign>
                        <lhs>
                            <output-ref id="rise"/>
                        </lhs>
                        <rhs>
                            <const>1'b0</const>
                        </rhs>
                    </assign>
                    <assign>
                        <lhs>
                            <output-ref id="down"/>
                        </lhs>
                        <rhs>
                            <const>1'b0</const>
                        </rhs>
                    </assign>
                </statement>
                <statement>
                    <text>Case statement for generating output signals based on current state and input a</text>
                    <case>
                        <switch>
                            <reg-ref id="i_state"/>
                            <enum-value id="ZERO"/>
                        </switch>
                        <case-item>
                            <condition>
                                <and>
                                    <input-ref id="a"/>
                                    <op>==</op>
                                    <const>1'b1</const>
                                </and>
                            </condition>
                            <statement>
                                <text>Set rise output signal to 1</text>
                                <assign>
                                    <lhs>
                                        <output-ref id="rise"/>
                                    </lhs>
                                    <rhs>
                                        <const>1'b1</const>
                                    </rhs>
                                </assign>
                            </statement>
                        </case-item>
                        <case-item>
                            <condition>
                                <and>
                                    <input-ref id="a"/>
                                    <op>==</op>
                                    <const>1'b0</const>
                                </and>
                            </condition>
                            <statement>
                                <text>Set down output signal to 1</text>
                                <assign>
                                    <lhs>
                                        <output-ref id="down"/>
                                    </lhs>
                                    <rhs>
                                        <const>1'b1</const>
                                    </rhs>
                                </assign>
                            </statement>
                        </case-item>
                    </case>
                </statement>
            </statement>
        </parablock>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier. Clock. Active-low reset. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Product. 4 The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding "size" number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
For example, if the input signal mul_a is 4'b1010, the extended signal will be 8'b0000_1010.
This is done so that the multiplication operation can be performed with the same logic for all bit positions.
In each iteration of the generate block, a multiplication operation is performed.
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier (mul_b) is 1, the multiplicand (mul_a) is left-shifted by the bit position (i) to perform the multiplication.
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0).
For example, if the multiplier bit is 0, the partial product is 0.
This is because any number multiplied by 0 is 0.
The partial products are added to get the final product.
The partial products are added in a staged manner.
The first stage adds the first two partial products, the third stage adds the result of the first stage to the third partial product.
This continues until all the partial products are added.
The final product is stored in the output signal (mul_out).
The output signal (mul_out) is updated with the final product in the positive edge of the clock or when the reset signal (rst_n) is low.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, the output signal (mul_out) is set to 0.
This is because all the stages are reset when the reset signal is low.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the output signal (mul_out) is updated with the final product.
The final product is the sum of all the partial products.

Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller. Write clock. Read clock. Reset signal. Write increment. Read increment. Write data. Write full. Read empty. Read data. Data width of FIFO. Depth of FIFO.

Implement an asynchronous FIFO with depth and data width .

Asynchronous FIFO is composed of four parts:

  1. Dual-port RAM for data storage.

  2. Data write controller.

  3. Data read controller.

  4. Read pointer synchronizer.

  5. Write pointer synchronizer.

For empty and full judgment, compare the gray code of read and write pointers.

samples.xml_description:
Triangle Wave signal generator. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Output waveform signal. Internal state for waveform generation. The module includes a register, state, used to control the state of the waveform generation.
The module includes a register, wave, with a width of 5 bits, which represents the generated waveform.
The state and waveform registers are updated in the always block, triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).
On reset, indicated by ~rst_n, the state register is cleared to 0, and the wave register is cleared to 0.
The waveform generation is controlled using a case statement based on the current state:
If the state is 0, the waveform (wave) is incremented by 1.
If the waveform reaches 31 (wave == 31), the state is transitioned to 1.
If the state is 1, the waveform is decremented by 1.
If the waveform reaches 0 (wave == 0), the state is transitioned back to 0.
The waveform generation continues to cycle between 0 and 31 based on the state transitions.
 endmodule

<module id="adder_pipe_64bit">
    <description>Implement a module of a 64-bit ripple carry adder, which includes several registers to enable the pipeline stages.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active low reset signal</input>
        <input id="i_en">Enable signal for addition operation</input>
        <input id="adda" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand A</input>
        <input id="addb" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand B</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[64:0]">65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb.</output>
        <output id="o_en">Output enable signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="PIPELINE_STAGES" value="3">Number of pipeline stages in the adder.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <reg id="i_en_reg" value="0">Register to store the input enable signal.</reg>
            <reg id="adda_reg" width_description="[63:0]">Register to store the input operand A.</reg>
            <reg id="addb_reg" width_description="[63:0]">Register to store the input operand B.</reg>
            <reg id="o_en_reg" value="0">Register to store the output enable signal.</reg>
            <reg id="sum_reg" width_description="[64:0]">Register to store the sum of adda and addb.</reg>
            <always @("posedge clk or negedge rst_n")>
                <if condition="!rst_n">
                    <assign>i_en_reg = 0;</assign>
                    <assign>adda_reg = 0;</assign>
                    <assign>addb_reg = 0;</assign>
                    <assign>o_en_reg = 0;</assign>
                    <assign>sum_reg = 0;</assign>
                </if>
                <else>
                    <assign>i_en_reg = i_en;</assign>
                    <assign>adda_reg = adda;</assign>
                    <assign>addb_reg = addb;</assign>
                    <assign>o_en_reg = o_en_reg;</assign>
                    <if condition="i_en_reg">
                        <assign>sum_reg = adda_reg + addb_reg + o_en_reg;</assign>
                    </if>
                </else>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <assign>o_en = o_en_reg;</assign>
        <assign>result = sum_reg;</assign>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for pulse detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </input>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">Initial state of the pulse detection process.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State indicating the end of a pulse.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="data_out_reg">Store the output signal.</sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="state">Store the current state of the pulse detection process.</sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module pulse_detect detects pulses in the input signal data_in. A pulse is defined as a sequence of 1s and 0s, where the pulse is considered to be present when data_in changes from 0 to 1 to 0 for 3 cycles. The output signal data_out is 1 at the end of the 3-cycle pulse and 0 otherwise.
    The module has a finite state machine (FSM) with four states: S0, S1, S2, and S3. The FSM transitions between these states based on the value of data_in and the current state. The output signal data_out is set to 1 when the FSM is in state S3, indicating the end of a 3-cycle pulse. The FSM is reset to state S0 when the reset signal rst_n is asserted (active low).
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate 4 input data and output the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="idle" value="0">Idle state, waiting for valid_in.</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="accumulate" value="1">Accumulate state, ready to receive data_in.</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="valid_out_reg" type="sequential_logic">A register to store the valid_out value.</reg>
        <reg id="data_out_reg" width_description="[9:0]" type="sequential_logic">A register to store the data_out value.</reg>
        <reg id="state_reg" width_description="[1:0]" type="sequential_logic">A register to store the state value.</reg>
        <reg id="count_reg" width_description="[1:0]" type="sequential_logic">A register to store the count value.</reg>
        <reg id="sum_reg" width_description="[9:0]" type="sequential_logic">A register to store the sum value.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <always_comb>
            <case>
                <case-item id="accumulate">
                    <if>
                        <condition>count_reg == 2'b11.</condition>
                        <then>
                            <assign>valid_out_reg = 1'b1;</assign>
                            <assign>data_out_reg = sum_reg;</assign>
                            <assign>state_reg = idle;</assign>
                        </then>
                        <else>
                            <assign>valid_out_reg = 1'b0;</assign>
                            <assign>data_out_reg = data_out_reg;</assign>
                            <assign>state_reg = accumulate;</assign>
                        </else>
                    </if>
                </case-item>
                <case-item id="idle">
                    <if>
                        <condition>valid_in == 1'b1.</condition>
                        <then>
                            <assign>valid_out_reg = 1'b0;</assign>
                            <assign>data_out_reg = data_out_reg;</assign>
                            <assign>state_reg = accumulate;</assign>
                        </then>
                    </if>
                </case-item>
            </case>
        </always_comb>
        <always_ff>
            <for-each>
                <target id="valid_out" value="valid_out_reg" />
                <target id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]" value="data_out_reg" />
                <target id="state" width_description="[1:0]" value="state_reg" />
                <target id="count" width_description="[1:0]" value="count_reg" />
                <target id="sum" width_description="[9:0]" value="sum_reg" />
            </for-each>
        </always_ff>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. Width of the data. Depth of the RAM.

Define a 8*6 RAM array.

reg [DEPTH - 1 : 0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0];

Trigger the always block on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)

Reset the RAM array when the reset signal is active (indicated by !rst_n).

if (!rst_n) begin for (int i = 0; i < 2**WIDTH; i++) begin RAM[i] <= 6'b000000; end end

Write data to the RAM array at the specified address (write_addr) when the write enable signal (write_en) is active.

else if (write_en) begin RAM[write_addr] <= write_data; end

Trigger the always block on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)

Reset the read_data register when the reset signal is active (indicated by !rst_n).

if (!rst_n) begin read_data <= 6'b000000; end

Read data from the RAM array at the specified address (read_addr) when the read enable signal (read_en) is active.

else if (read_en) begin read_data <= RAM[read_addr]; end

Clear the read_data register when the read enable signal is not active.

else begin read_data <= 6'b000000; end

Implement a right shifter. Clock. Input signal to be right-shifted. Output signal representing the result of the right shift operation. Width of the input and output signals. sequential_logic The clock signal that triggers updates to the output on each rising edge. The input signal to be right-shifted. The output signal representing the result of the right shift operation.

Initialize the q register to 0 using the initial statement.

On each rising edge of the clock, right-shift the contents of the q register by 1 bit using the >> operator.

Insert the new input bit d into the most significant position of the register.

samples.xml_description:
<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer, data_in will remain constant during the period when data_en is high, and data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b clock cycles. When the value of data_en is high, data can be synchronized. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum_value id="idle" value="2'b00">Idle state.</enum_value>
            <enum_value id="wait_en" value="2'b01">Wait for enable signal.</enum_value>
            <enum_value id="sync_data" value="2'b10">Synchronize data.</enum_value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="data_reg" width_description="[3:0]" type="sequential_logic">Register to store input data.</reg>
        <reg id="en_data_reg" width_description="[3:0]" type="sequential_logic">Register to store enable data.</reg>
        <reg id="en_clap_one" width_description="[3:0]" type="sequential_logic">Enable control register.</reg>
        <reg id="en_clap_two" width_description="[3:0]" type="sequential_logic">Enable control register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module includes a register, data_reg, to store the input data signal, data_in. On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register. If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the register (data_reg) is set to 0. If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (data_reg) is updated with the input data signal (data_in).
    The module includes a register, en_data_reg, to store the input enable signal, data_en. On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register. If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, the register (en_data_reg) is set to 0. If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (en_data_reg) is updated with the input enable signal (data_en).
    The module includes two registers, en_clap_one and en_clap_two, to control the selection of the input data. On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module updates the registers. If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, both registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are set to 0. If the reset signal B (brstn) is high, the registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are updated based on the value of en_data_reg. The register en_clap_one is assigned the value of en_data_reg, and en_clap_two is assigned the previous value of en_clap_one.
    On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module assigns the output data value. If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the output data (dataout) is set to 0. If the reset signal B (brstn) is high and the control signal (en_clap_two) is active, the output data (dataout) is assigned the value of the data register (data_reg). If the control signal (en_clap_two) is inactive, the output data (dataout) retains its previous value.
    </implementation>
</module>
